title: Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (RACGP)
metaTitle: RACGP for IMGs: FRACGP & SIMG Assessment
metaDescription: How the RACGP assesses specialist IMGs for FRACGP: the comparability assessment, the Practice Experience Program Specialist, exams, and the expedited pathway.

collegeMeta.fullName: Royal Australian College of General Practitioners

summaryMarkdown: The Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (RACGP) is the specialist college for general practice in Australia. Its Fellowship, the FRACGP, is a specialist GP qualification accredited by the AMC. [[cite:fracgp-amc-accredited]] For specialist international medical graduates (SIMGs) who trained as GPs overseas, the RACGP runs a comparability assessment with three possible outcomes: substantially comparable, partially comparable or not comparable. [[cite:racgp-comparability-three-outcomes]] General practice is also on the Medical Board's [Expedited Specialist pathway](/pathways/expedited-specialist), which is run by the Board (not the RACGP) and can lead to FRACGP once specialist registration is granted. [[cite:gp-on-expedited-list]]

sections.heading: What the RACGP and FRACGP are
sections.bodyMarkdown: The RACGP is responsible for general practice training, standards and assessment in Australia. Its Fellowship, the FRACGP, is a specialist GP qualification accredited by the AMC. [[cite:fracgp-amc-accredited]] For an overseas-trained GP, being assessed as comparable to that standard is what underpins recognition as a specialist GP. The RACGP assesses how your overseas qualifications and experience compare, then maps them onto a training-and-assessment pathway to close any gaps before Fellowship. [[cite:racgp-comparability-definition]]

sections.heading: The comparability assessment
sections.bodyMarkdown:
SIMGs on the specialist pathway complete an RACGP **comparability assessment** measuring how comparable they are to an Australian-trained GP at the point of Fellowship. [[cite:racgp-comparability-definition]] It weighs your qualification, training route and curriculum, prior assessment method, GP experience, recency and continuity of practice, and CPD. [[cite:racgp-assessment-factors]] There are three outcomes: [[cite:racgp-comparability-three-outcomes]]

- **Substantially comparable**, broadly equivalent to the Australian standard.
- **Partially comparable**, gaps to close through further training and examination.
- **Not comparable**, does not currently meet the standard for this pathway.

A comparability outcome is valid for 12 months from the outcome letter, and partially/not comparable applicants may re-apply after a material change in training and experience. [[cite:racgp-outcome-validity]]

sections.heading: Practice experience and exams
sections.bodyMarkdown: Substantially or partially comparable SIMGs enter the **Practice Experience Program Specialist (PEP Specialist)**, supervised general practice in an RACGP-approved post with workplace-based assessment and core learning. [[cite:pep-specialist-program]] **Substantially comparable** SIMGs are not required to sit the FRACGP exams and complete a shorter program before a Fellowship recommendation. [[cite:substantially-no-exams]] **Partially comparable** SIMGs must pass the RACGP Fellowship exams, the Applied Knowledge Test (AKT), the Key Feature Problem (KFP) and the Clinical Competency Exam (CCE). [[cite:partially-must-sit-exams]] The RACGP also sets minimum eligibility thresholds, including a defined period of GP experience and recent practice/CPD. [[cite:racgp-eligibility-thresholds]] Confirm current rules with the RACGP.

sections.heading: General practice and the expedited pathway
sections.bodyMarkdown: General practice is one of the specialties on the Medical Board's [Expedited Specialist pathway](/pathways/expedited-specialist), for SIMGs whose qualifications the Board assesses as substantially equivalent. [[cite:gp-on-expedited-list]] This pathway is run entirely by the Medical Board and Ahpra, not the RACGP. [[cite:expedited-run-by-mba]] Once an SIMG completes the Board's pathway and holds unconditional specialist registration in general practice, they can apply for Fellowship of the RACGP and use the post-nominals FRACGP. [[cite:expedited-to-fracgp]] See the [Specialist pathway](/pathways/specialist) for how the routes compare.

claims.text: The FRACGP is a specialist general practice qualification accredited by the Australian Medical Council.
The RACGP comparability assessment determines how comparable a specialist IMG is to an Australian-trained GP at the point of admission to Fellowship.
The assessment considers the SIMG's GP qualification, training route and curriculum, prior assessment method, GP experience, recency and continuity of practice, and CPD.
A RACGP comparability assessment results in one of three outcomes: substantially comparable, partially comparable, or not comparable.
A comparability outcome is valid for 12 months from the outcome letter, and partially/not comparable applicants may re-apply after a material change in training and experience.
Substantially or partially comparable SIMGs enter the Practice Experience Program Specialist, supervised general practice in an RACGP-approved post with workplace-based assessment and core learning.
SIMGs assessed as substantially comparable are not required to sit the FRACGP examinations.
Partially comparable SIMGs must pass the RACGP Fellowship exams, the Applied Knowledge Test, the Key Feature Problem and the Clinical Competency Exam.
The RACGP sets minimum eligibility thresholds for the comparability assessment, including a defined period of GP experience and recent practice and CPD.
General practice is one of the specialties accepted on the Medical Board's Expedited Specialist pathway.
The Expedited Specialist pathway is run entirely by the Medical Board of Australia and Ahpra, not the RACGP.
After completing the Board's Expedited Specialist pathway and holding unconditional specialist registration in general practice, a doctor can apply for Fellowship of the RACGP.

faq.question: What is the FRACGP?
faq.answerMarkdown: Fellowship of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners, a specialist general practice qualification accredited by the AMC, which underpins recognition as a specialist GP in Australia.

faq.question: How does the RACGP assess overseas-trained GPs?
faq.answerMarkdown: Through a comparability assessment that measures how closely your training and experience match an Australian-trained GP at the point of Fellowship, considering your qualification, training, prior assessment, GP experience, recency and CPD.

faq.question: Do SIMGs have to sit the RACGP Fellowship exams?
faq.answerMarkdown: It depends on the outcome. Substantially comparable SIMGs are not required to sit them; partially comparable SIMGs must pass the AKT, KFP and CCE before Fellowship.

faq.question: Is general practice on the Expedited Specialist pathway?
faq.answerMarkdown: Yes. General practice is accepted on the Medical Board's Expedited Specialist pathway, run by the Board and Ahpra (not the RACGP). After completing it and gaining specialist registration, a doctor can apply for FRACGP.

faq.question: How long is an RACGP comparability outcome valid?
faq.answerMarkdown: 12 months from the date of the outcome letter. Requirements can change, so confirm the current rules with the RACGP.
